description: Universal SAT (Systematic Approach to Tasting) structure templates for distinction-chain evaluation

quality_signals: viscosity correlates with alcohol and extract

primary.description: Grape variety and fermentation aromas (fresh fruit, flowers, spice)

secondary.description: Winemaking and aging aromas (oak, yeast, butter, toast)

tertiary.description: Oxidative and bottle-age aromas (leather, tobacco, dried fruits, honey)

quality_signals: aromatic intensity suggests ripeness and fruit quality
balance of primary, secondary, tertiary indicates complexity and age-ability

quality_indicators: high acidity in cool climate wines indicates authenticity
low acidity in white wines suggests poor vintage or over-ripeness
harsh tannins indicate under-ripeness or poor extraction

alcohol_assessment.balance_factor: alcohol should be in balance with fruit, acid, and tannin

body_assessment.correlation: determined by alcohol, tannin, and extract

quality_signals: fruit-driven flavors indicate freshness and quality
integrated secondary flavors suggest good winemaking

finish_assessment.quality_correlation: longer finish indicates higher quality and aging potential

outstanding.definition: Exceptional wine showing excellent concentration, complexity, and balance

excellent.definition: High-quality wine with strong concentration and complexity

very_good.definition: Good quality wine, well-made, pleasant to drink

good.definition: Decent, drinkable wine with basic quality

readiness.description: Assessment of how long the wine will age and develop

primary_descriptors.category: Primary aromas from grape variety and fermentation
primary_descriptors.quality_indication: Primary descriptors should dominate young wines and indicate variety authenticity

secondary_descriptors.category: Secondary aromas from malolactic fermentation and oak aging
secondary_descriptors.quality_indication: Secondary descriptors in balance with primary indicate good winemaking

tertiary_descriptors.category: Tertiary aromas from bottle aging and oxidation
tertiary_descriptors.quality_indication: Tertiary descriptors in appropriate wines indicate potential for aging

completeness_checklist: Appearance assessed with color, clarity, viscosity
Nose assessed with intensity and character
Palate assessed with acidity, tannin (if red), alcohol, body, flavor, finish
distinction_indicators: All five sections present and substantive
Quality assessment well-reasoned with specific evidence
Readiness assessment shows understanding of aging potential
common_issues_to_avoid: Incomplete SAT sections (missing appearance, nose, or palate detail)
Generic quality statement without supporting evidence
Confusing varietal character with descriptor categorization
Failure to balance positive and negative observations

appearance_coaching.if_missing_color: Describe what you see: is it pale, medium, or deep? What hue? (gold, amber, ruby, garnet)
appearance_coaching.if_missing_clarity: Is the wine bright and clear, or hazy? Any suspended particles?
appearance_coaching.if_missing_viscosity: Watch how the wine flows down the glass - is it thin or viscous?
appearance_coaching.if_superficial: Move beyond just stating color - explain what color tells you about the wine

nose_coaching.if_closed: Sometimes young wines smell closed. Try warming the glass or giving it time
nose_coaching.if_one_descriptor: You identified one aroma. What else do you smell? Primary, secondary, or tertiary?
nose_coaching.if_generic: Instead of 'fruity', be specific: what kind of fruit? (citrus, stone, red, dark?)
nose_coaching.if_missing_intensity: Rate the intensity: closed, restrained, moderate, pronounced, or powerful?

palate_coaching.if_missing_acidity: Every wine has acidity. Is it sharp/crisp or soft/low?
palate_coaching.if_missing_tannin: For reds: are there tannins? Firm, ripe, harsh, or absent?
palate_coaching.if_missing_body: Consider alcohol level and weight on the palate. Light, medium, or full?
palate_coaching.if_no_finish: Swallow and see how long flavors persist. Short, medium, or long?
palate_coaching.if_unbalanced_view: Consider all dimensions together. Do they balance? Or is something dominating?

quality_coaching.if_missing_evidence: State your quality judgment, then explain what in the wine supports it
quality_coaching.if_vague: Instead of 'good', be specific: very good because of clear terroir expression
quality_coaching.if_all_negative: All wines have some merit. What works well? What needs improvement?
quality_coaching.if_no_reasoning: Quality is about balance and expression. What does this wine express well?

readiness_coaching.if_missing: Consider: acidity, tannin structure, alcohol, concentration. Can it age?
readiness_coaching.if_vague: Not just 'can age' - estimate how long: 5 years, 10 years, 20+ years?
readiness_coaching.if_inconsistent: If you said it's outstanding, why would it only age 2 years?
readiness_coaching.if_ignoring_key_factor: You have good acidity - that suggests aging potential. Explain
